title: Racosperma crombiei
taxonomy: Racosperma crombiei is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to Australia and is also known as Acacia crombiei. Racosperma crombiei was previously classified under the genus Acacia, but it was reclassified in 2001 into the genus Racosperma. However, some sources may still refer to it as Acacia crombiei. The exact classification of Racosperma crombiei can vary depending on the source and taxonomic system being used.

The plant has been traditionally used in Western Australia for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in wound healing and the treatment of infections.
